summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrates host metadata to hardcoded Cloudflare Worker endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` hook runs `node index.js`, which performs an unconditional HTTPS POST to `https://npx-canary-log.vulnerable-live.workers.dev/log` carrying the installer's hostname (`os.hostname()`), current working directory (`process.cwd()`), npm user-agent (`process.env.npm_config_user_agent`), Node version, platform, and architecture. The endpoint is hardcoded at index.js:18; the data is collected and transmitted at lines 20-29. There is no opt-out, no disclosure prompt, and the destination is an author-controlled Cloudflare Worker rather than a documented vendor or publisher endpoint. The package name impersonates a Sentry MCP server, increasing the chance of accidental installation. Even if framed as research, every installer is fingerprinted and deanonymized (hostname, build path, CI vs developer UA) without consent at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
